# Pipaflow

> Brutalist flow-diagram editor that runs entirely in the browser.
> Saves and loads `.json` files with a documented schema, so an LLM can
> generate diagrams that import directly via the "Deschide" button.

## Quick spec

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "name": "string",
  "nodes": [{
    "id": "unique-string",
    "type": "box | sticky | decision | terminal",
    "data": {
      "label": "short text",
      "variant": "start | end (only for type=terminal)",
      "fillColor": { "bg": "#hex", "fg": "#hex", "border": "#hex" }
    },
    "position": { "x": 300, "y": 60 }
  }],
  "edges": [{
    "id": "unique-string",
    "source": "node-id",
    "target": "node-id",
    "sourceHandle": "yes | no (only for decision-source edges)",
    "label": "optional short text on the line",
    "animated": false,
    "style": { "strokeWidth": 2.5, "stroke": "#0a0a0a", "strokeDasharray": "8 4" },
    "markerEnd": { "type": "arrowclosed", "color": "#0a0a0a", "width": 18, "height": 18 }
  }]
}
```

## Node types

- `terminal` — start/end pill (`data.variant: "start" | "end"`)
- `box` — rectangle action (most common)
- `decision` — diamond fork. Outgoing edges should set `sourceHandle: "yes" | "no"` to pick which side
- `sticky` — yellow handwritten note (commentary only, no logic)

## Fill color presets

Use any of these for `data.fillColor`:

| Preset | bg | fg |
|---|---|---|
| Snow   | #fafafa | #0a0a0a |
| Ash    | #e5e5e5 | #0a0a0a |
| Butter | #fff8c5 | #0a0a0a |
| Flame  | #ea580c | #ffffff |
| Blush  | #fce7e8 | #0a0a0a |
| Danger | #e11d3f | #ffffff |
| Wine   | #7a0e22 | #ffffff |
| Toxic  | #84cc16 | #0a0a0a |
| Noir   | #0a0a0a | #ffffff |

(All presets share `border: "#0a0a0a"`.)

## Edge styles

| Visual | strokeDasharray | animated |
|---|---|---|
| Solid (default) | omit | false |
| Dashed          | "8 4" | false |
| Dotted          | "2 4" | false |
| Marching ants   | "8 4" | true |

Bidirectional edges set both `markerEnd` and `markerStart`.

## Layout

- Top-down: start ~y=60, +120 per row, x≈300 center
- Decision branches: "yes" right, "no" left (mirror by x offset of ±200)
- The app has Dagre auto-layout, so rough positions are fine

## Output rules

- Return ONLY the JSON object (no markdown fences, no commentary)
- Every `source`/`target` must reference an existing node id
- Use camelCase exactly (`fillColor`, not `fill_color`; `sourceHandle`, not `source_handle`)
- Keep node labels under 60 chars; long descriptions go in `sticky` nodes

## Repo

https://github.com/Radu-Podila/pipaflow
